stories.title: Israel is building a buffer zone inside Lebanon
stories.description: Israel is creating a large buffer zone in southern Lebanon for a prolonged military occupation, with low expectations that direct talks with Lebanon will lead to quick action on disarming Hezbollah.

stories.title: Veteran diplomat offers insights into the war in Iran — and thoughts on what's next
stories.description: The war entered a new phase when President Trump began a U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Aaron David Miller of the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace explains what this means.

stories.title: Fuel protests have Ireland's government facing possible no-confidence vote
stories.description: The prime minister announced new tax cuts to try to end the crisis that began after the U.S.-Israel war on Iran led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The government could face a no-confidence vote over its response to the fuel protests.

stories.title: Eric Swalwell will resign from Congress. And, Trump feuds with Pope Leo over Iran War
stories.description: Eric Swalwell is resigning from Congress after multiple women accused him of sexual assault and misconduct. And, Trump is feuding with Pope Leo, calling him weak on crime for opposing the war.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to start peace negotiations after rare talks in D.C.
stories.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the talks were meant to lay out a framework for lasting peace and to bring an end to Hezbollah's influence in the region.

stories.title: Ukraine hopes for more cooperation after Hungary elects new prime minister
stories.description: The incoming prime minister of Hungary is no fan of Ukraine, but he says he wants to work with the European Union, raising hopes he'll lift a Hungarian veto on a $100 billion EU loan to Kyiv.

stories.title: 'Hanging by a thread': Fertilizer shortage in India deepens due to ongoing Iran war
stories.description: With shipments through the Strait of Hormuz stalled after the Iran war began, fertilizer shortages are deepening across India.

stories.title: Lebanon seeks peace, but Hezbollah needs to be convinced first
stories.description: The Lebanese government go into peace talks with limited influence over the group.

stories.title: 'They are striking everywhere' - Paramedics in Lebanon speak to BBC after Israeli strikes
stories.description: BBC Middle East correspondent Hugo Bachega joins paramedics on duty in Nabatieh - a city that was once vibrant, but is now abandoned.

stories.title: Israelis war-weary but most oppose Iran ceasefire, poll suggests
stories.description: The vast majority did not believe Hezbollah or Iran had been severely weakened by weeks of Israeli and US attacks, according to the poll.

stories.title: The US blockade of Iran is a gamble. Will it work?
stories.description: The US military is gambling on the blockade denying Iran vital trade - but questions remain on whether the bet will pay off.

stories.title: Israel steps up strikes on Lebanon and Hezbollah attacks continue as talks loom
stories.description: More than 160 people have been killed since Friday in Lebanon, where Israel is pressing on with its campaign against Hezbollah despite the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.title: Iran carried out highest number of executions in decades
stories.description: Organisations warn many more people could be executed in the wake of the anti-government protests which swept Iran earlier this year.

stories.title: Starmer says UK will not join Trump's blockade of Iran's ports
stories.description: UK minesweepers and anti-drone capabilities will continue operating in the region, as pressure ramps up to reopen the key shipping route.

stories.title: After Iran talks falter, the big question is what happens next?
stories.description: Twenty-one hours was not enough to end 47 years of hostility between Iran and the US, writes the BBC's Lyse Doucet.

stories.title: We spoke to the man making viral Lego-style AI videos for Iran. Experts say it's powerful propaganda
stories.description: "Slopaganda" is too weak a term to capture how powerful this "highly sophisticated" content is, one expert says.

stories.title: Concerns after satellite provider restricts Iran images following US pressure
stories.description: Planet Labs says it is restricting coverage of Iran and parts of the Middle East indefinitely.

stories.title: Ambassadors meet for historic talks in US amid deeply fraught Lebanon-Israel tensions
stories.description: Eve Irvine is pleased to welcome Simon Mabon, Professor of International Politics and Director of the Richardson Institute for Peace Studies at Lancaster University, specialising in Middle East studies. He is also Director of SEPAD (the Sectarianism, Proxies and De-Sectarianizati...

stories.title: Middle East live: Trump says Iran talks may resume as Israel, Lebanon open direct track
stories.description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday that US-Iran peace talks could resume this week, while Israel and Lebanon agreed to launch direct negotiations, signaling movement on two key fronts in efforts to ease the Middle East conflict. The parallel diplomatic openings came even as viol...

stories.title: 'War is development in reverse' UNDP chief tells France 24
stories.description: Amid uncertainty around the war in Iran, the International Monetary Fund downgraded its outlook for the global economy under three different scenarios depending on how long the conflict would last. Meanwhile, the United Nations Development Programme is warning that even if hostil...

stories.title: YouTube bans viral pro-Iran AI-generated LEGO videos trolling Trump
stories.description: As the ‘meme war’ between the US and Iran continues via AI ‘slopaganda,’ an Iran-linked group named Explosive Media has been pumping out viral LEGO-style videos ridiculing the US war effort in Iran, and trolling President Donald Trump. Many of these videos depict Trump as childis...

stories.title: Israeli-Lebanese negotiations 'largely symbolic and performative', Middle East expert says
stories.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Aaron David Miller, Senior Fellow at the Carnegie Endowment for Peace, says that "none of the participants in today's meetings have the authority to negotiate", adding that "implementing what the Lebanese and Israeli government would like to d...

stories.title: Middle East live: Israel, Lebanon agree to direct negotiations after DC meeting, US says
Israel, Lebanon united in liberating Lebanon from Hezbollah, Israeli ambassador to US says
stories.description: ⁠The ​Lebanese government made ​it clear during U.S.-brokered talks ​with ‌Israel ⁠that ‌they no longer want to ⁠be "occupied" by Hezbollah and ​that ‌there were conversations about long-term ‌vision for ​clearly delineated border, Israel's ambassador to the ​United States ​Yechi...

stories.title: Italian PM says suspends renewal of defence agreement with Israel
stories.description: Italy has suspended the renewal of its defence agreement with Israel, which involves the exchange of military equipment and technology research, Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni said Tuesday. US President Donald Trump criticised Meloni for her unwillingness to help in the Iran war, ...

stories.title: US military says blockade of Iran ports ‘completely’ halts economic trade
stories.description: US Central Command say more than 10,000 armed forces members, as well as warships and planes, are involved in blockade.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon hold direct talks for first time in decades
stories.description: After the first direct talks in decades, Israel and Lebanon have agreed to begin negotiations.

stories.title: Iran war live: Trump says war ‘close to over’ as Hormuz blockade continues
stories.description: US president says second round of talks could be held in Pakistan as naval blockade of Iranian ports continues.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon hold rare talks in Washington, DC, amid Iran war
stories.description: The meeting marks the first direct talks between the two sides, though US officials say more time is needed.

stories.title: How US blockade on Iran will worsen energy crisis
stories.description: With the US military blockade of Iranian ports now in force, global energy markets are already reacting.

stories.title: Why are global views of the US getting worse?
stories.description: We discuss the global standing of the US after launching war against Iran and years of worsening views of the nation.

stories.title: US hosts ‘rare’ direct talks between Lebanon and Israel
stories.description: • European nations, Rubio urge both sides to ‘seize the opportunity’ to end conflict• Joint statement says direct negotiations can pave way for lasting security• Lebanese president hopes meeting will ‘mark the beginning of ending the suffering’ as Beirut seeks ceasefire WASHINGTO...

stories.title: Pope walks in Augustine’s footsteps on Algeria trip
stories.description: ANNABA: Pope Leo XIV arrived on Tuesday in the Algerian city of Annaba, the birthplace of Christian theologian Saint Augustine, on day two of a historic trip marred by two suicide attacks. While the authorities have yet to comment, an informed source on Tuesday confirmed the bomb...

stories.title: China, Spain call for closer ties amid ‘crumbling’ world order
stories.description: BEIJING: Europe and China must forge closer ties to counter threats to multilateralism, Spanish Premier Pedro Sanchez said on Tuesday after meeting Chinese President Xi Jinping, who said the international order is “crumbling”. Sanchez’s April 11 to 15 visit to China comes as many...

stories.title: Trump says Italian PM Meloni lacks ‘courage’ on Iran war: report
stories.description: US President Donald Trump on Tuesday launched a stinging criticism of Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, one of his main European allies, over her unwillingness to join the Iran war. “I’m shocked at her. I thought she had courage, but I was wrong,” he said in an interview wit...

stories.title: Around 250 people feared missing after boat capsizes in Andaman Sea: UN
stories.description: Around 250 people, including children, were feared missing after a boat carrying Rohingya refugees and Bangladeshi nationals capsized in the Andaman Sea, the United Nations said on Tuesday. “The trawler, which departed from Teknaf in southern Bangladesh and was on its way to Mala...

stories.title: Around 90 detained in New York City protest over US arms sales to Israel
stories.description: Dozens of protesters were detained by police in New York City on Monday during demonstrations calling for the blocking of arms sales to Israel and an end to US military support for its ally. Demonstrators included the anti-war group Jewish Voice for Peace, which said around 90 pe...
